tokens: Okay, Pete, what is your next pleasure?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can press on along the traverse over to Sharp Crater.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Why don't you take your...Oh, you already got a snapshot of this, didn't you, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Calling, wanting to get the map before Al moves away with the HTC) Wait, wait, wait.
Sharp Crater, that's funny; I can't seem to locate it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, from your present position that's 400 feet southwest.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Moving) Al, it's got to be over that hill right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now we want to get the core tube and that gas sample and a bunch of good things, right, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: All those good things at Sharp Crater.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Al, you can go ahead and put that diverter valve to your choice.
Your feedwater pressure is holding even.
Looks as though it's working well.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Pause) Well, we're going in about the right direction.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's one right over here, kind of more to your right.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Trouble is, I'm looking down zero phase, you know, and that's...(Pause) There it is.
That's got to be it right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, there's some big fragments out here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Chuckles; Pause) No, that's not it (meaning Sharp Crater), either.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't we stop here and look at the chart a little bit more closely?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, does that LM look small back there.
I'd better get a tie anyhow.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Are you going to give us a backside survey at that point, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (We're so) darn far out, (so) I might as well.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: A full pan over when you get to Sharp.
We show you are 1 plus 23 into the EVA and we're looking to leave Sharp Crater around 1 plus 51, so you got lots of time.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We got to find Sharp Crater first.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Responding to Gibson) Gee, I kind of agree with you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It looks to me to be about 30 meters.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's right here in front of me.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This has got to be Sharp Crater right here.
We'll drive that double core tube in the...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This has a nice white rim on it.
In fact, the rim of this looks pretty much like the area we kicked over on the previous craters.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm not sure this is Sharp Crater, but it's ...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's use it anyway, because it's the only one out here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're estimating a diameter of Sharp Crater, Pete, for about 40 feet (12 meters).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It's got a nice raised rim on it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's raised up about, what'd you say, 2 feet?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The trouble is that I'm running zero phase.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, this is the same color as all that subsurface material.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's awful soft in here; watch it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Look at the bottom of that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It looks like blast effect coming out of it.
Looks like it's got blast effects radial all around.
This has got to be fairly fresh to the...Hey, look at that, Al.
Wait 'til I get some pictures of that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't know what to set it on.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, the rim is soft here, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Quite a bit softer than the others we...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But look at the radial spray pattern.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I guess I'm supposed to drive the, what, double core tube here or something?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We got to dig a trench...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, we'd like to get the trench site sample there, and you can hold off on that double core tube until you get over to Halo Crater.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Pause) Aren't we supposed to look west for Copernican rays here, too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, there's no way to tell a difference contact-wise.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now this one is fresh enough so that you can see - like you say - some of the rays (produced by Sharp itself).
But (with) any crater older than this, there doesn't appear to be any way to tell the materials from inside the crater from that (material) that was right on the surface before the crater was formed.
Which sample do you want now?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, we're looking for the trench site sample.
That includes your environmental sample of trench and the gas analyses you can put in there, too.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We'll do the whole smash here for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want it right in the crater rim?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That would be perhaps the easiest and best place to do it, and you can get that one core tube down in the bottom of the trench.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Before you do that, you're going to have to lift this up so that I can take the sample out.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You going to do it right there?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay; lift it up and I'll reach in there and grab the...Get the...This (large can) will be the one for the soil here.
(Pause) Hey, one thing I've noticed, Houston, carrying the tools.
Although your gloves...(Re-starting the thought) You don't feel any of the temperature here.
Sun's out nice and bright, but it's nice and cool in here (in the suit); except when you're carrying something metal, like the Hand Tool Carrier, or the shovel, or something.
Then your hand starts to get warm.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Could you get out of the...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Could you just slide a little bit?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me slide right over here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I've got to do it over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Did you take a picture before, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (To Conrad) I'll take one right now, Pete.
(Pause) That'd be a good spot right there, I believe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You could drive three core tubes down there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete's digging a nice clean trench.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me get the trench pictures.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And I'm in the wrong setting, too...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Could we have some numbers along with those pictures?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We'll have to give them to you, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Take a look at my...What's the (
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're on number 105 (out of about 180 frames on the magazine).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll trade cameras with you because you've been (garbled under Pete).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now what do we want to do?
Fill that with dirt and rocks?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Fill the big container with dirt.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now I can't see the trench.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And when you get it in there, try to tap it (that is, tap the scoop on the top of the can).
Maybe it will come out by itself.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You got to come this way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: See, what's happening is my arm is being turned by the cable.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Maybe you could grab the handles closer to the thingamajig (that is, the head of the tool) there...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) get half of it that way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Chuckling) Well, you still need some more, although one more scoop ought to do it though.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're getting close to the crater.
(Both laughing) You get about 10 percent in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want to do one more?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: One more and you'll make it.
That soil just doesn't...There isn't anything holding it together.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Still laughing) It takes me back in my childhood when, you know, you wanted to fling things in every direction (out of frustration).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause; Pete's laughter subsides) Okay; now let me put the lid on.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Starts laughing again; trying to control himself) I'm sorry, but I just (garbled) put dirt in the tube.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Can I help you with that?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll hold this and you get the lid.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, you put the lid on.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Houston, this dirt came from about 8 inches down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Eight inches down, and what's the sample bag number on that?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: No, this is the deep trench sample in the...(To Al) What's the matter?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We've done this a zillion times.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now you hold it nice and tight.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll tell you what's the matter; you're getting friction.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hold her tight and I'll get it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Suppose you're getting some vacuum welding?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Will you snap the top off?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's a good top in that one.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, you need a core tube in the bottom of that trench.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Al, when you get a chance can we get your photo numbers?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And this is core tube number 2.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Core tube 2 and I'll need the (extension handle to attach to the top of the core tube)...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ought to be a good place, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: In this kind of pack you could almost drive it without a hammer; but, if you'll hand it (the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I want to take a couple more shots (that is, photos) of this before we leave.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get it all the way in (and) I'll get the pictures.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (The sound of hammering is audible) It's driving in real easy, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't lean down too far now.
And we're driving it all the way in pretty easy.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's put this up (that is, put the hammer back on the HTC).
Let me take a picture of it, Pete.
Make sure we got it documented.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Boy, this dirt's gotten on my camera and I can't see the settings anymore.
I'm going to have to do something about that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You ready to put the top on this core tube?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pulling it slowly out of the ground) Here we come.
(Pause) I hope that soil stays in there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Probably did because it stayed in your scoop so well.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Get the scoop back on (the extension handle).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: What else do you want here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We show you should have gotten in the trench site sample, the core tube samples from the bottom and also the gas analysis sample.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We need some little rock fragments for that (gas analysis sample), Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We're going to get it; hold the phone.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled, possibly as he strains forward to see into the bag)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll move it around; see if you see it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That a boy; you ought to have it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (We need to put) some little rocks in here (in the gas sample)...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You got some rocks in the gas analysis (sample); and would you also confirm that you have gotten the environmental sample?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We got the environmental sample, we got...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) And I'm trying to find a little rock.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Ho-ho, just right for that little can.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, your PLSS feedwater is back up to nominal and all looks good.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Pete) See those bright shiny ones there?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yup, yup; yup, yup, yup, yup.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's get a shot of them.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, that's a neat ...Ooh, that's glass!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Make a good sample for them.
(Pause) And that piece right next to it, there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, how far are we from the LM?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, we need some more, Pete.
There's not enough in there to do anything with.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Chuckling) Hey, come on, I'm getting tired of picking up those little things!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can't - Where is my stupid tool (the tongs?)?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I don't think that will fit.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete and Al, we show you 1200 feet from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Pause) Come on, Al, we're wasting time.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, as soon as you finish up there, you can head on back toward the east, towards Halo Crater.
No need to go any further west.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Hey, good show, Pete; good turning.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Is the front of my lens clean?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Give me one gnomon and my shovel.
transcript.speaker: Conrad
